root_text.an4.193:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.an4.193:1.2: Atha kho bhaddiyo licchavi yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho bhaddiyo licchavi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.193:2.2: ‘māyāvī samaṇo gotamo āvaṭṭaniṁ māyaṁ jānāti yāya aññatitthiyānaṁ sāvake āvaṭṭetī’ti.
root_text.an4.193:2.4: ‘māyāvī samaṇo gotamo āvaṭṭaniṁ māyaṁ jānāti yāya aññatitthiyānaṁ sāvake āvaṭṭetī’ti, kacci te, bhante, bhagavato vuttavādino, na ca bhagavantaṁ abhūtena abbhācikkhanti, dhammassa ca anudhammaṁ byākaronti, na ca koci sahadhammiko vādānupāto gārayhaṁ ṭhānaṁ āgacchati, anabbhakkhātukāmā hi mayaṁ, bhante, bhagavantan”ti?
root_text.an4.193:3.1: “Etha tumhe, bhaddiya, mā anussavena, mā paramparāya, mā itikirāya, mā piṭakasampadānena, mā takkahetu, mā nayahetu, mā ākāraparivitakkena, mā diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā, mā bhabbarūpatāya, mā ‘samaṇo no garū’ti.
root_text.an4.193:3.3: ‘ime dhammā akusalā, ime dhammā sāvajjā, ime dhammā viññugarahitā, ime dhammā samattā samādinnā ahitāya dukkhāya saṁvattantī’ti, atha tumhe, bhaddiya, pajaheyyātha.
root_text.an4.193:4.2: lobho purisassa ajjhattaṁ uppajjamāno uppajjati hitāya vā ahitāya vā”ti?
root_text.an4.193:4.4: “Luddho panāyaṁ, bhaddiya, purisapuggalo lobhena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto pāṇampi hanati, adinnampi ādiyati, paradārampi gacchati, musāpi bhaṇati, parampi tathattāya samādapeti yaṁsa hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāyā”ti.
root_text.an4.193:5.2: doso purisassa …pe… moho purisassa …pe… sārambho purisassa ajjhattaṁ uppajjamāno uppajjati hitāya vā ahitāya vā”ti?
root_text.an4.193:5.4: “Sāraddho panāyaṁ, bhaddiya, purisapuggalo sārambhena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto pāṇampi hanati, adinnampi ādiyati, paradārampi gacchati, musāpi bhaṇati, parampi tathattāya samādapeti yaṁsa hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāyā”ti.
root_text.an4.193:6.1: “Taṁ kiṁ maññatha, bhaddiya, ime dhammā kusalā vā akusalā vā”ti?
root_text.an4.193:6.7: “Samattā samādinnā ahitāya dukkhāya saṁvattanti, no vā?
root_text.an4.193:7.1: “Iti kho, bhaddiya, yaṁ taṁ te avocumhā—
root_text.an4.193:7.2: etha tumhe, bhaddiya, mā anussavena, mā paramparāya, mā itikirāya, mā piṭakasampadānena, mā takkahetu, mā nayahetu, mā ākāraparivitakkena, mā diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā, mā bhabbarūpatāya, mā ‘samaṇo no garū’ti.
root_text.an4.193:7.4: ‘ime dhammā akusalā, ime dhammā sāvajjā, ime dhammā viññugarahitā, ime dhammā samattā samādinnā ahitāya dukkhāya saṁvattantīti, atha tumhe, bhaddiya, pajaheyyāthā’ti,
root_text.an4.193:7.5: iti yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ idametaṁ paṭicca vuttaṁ.
root_text.an4.193:8.1: Etha tumhe, bhaddiya, mā anussavena, mā paramparāya, mā itikirāya, mā piṭakasampadānena, mā takkahetu, mā nayahetu, mā ākāraparivitakkena, mā diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā, mā bhabbarūpatāya, mā ‘samaṇo no garū’ti.
root_text.an4.193:8.3: ‘ime dhammā kusalā, ime dhammā anavajjā, ime dhammā viññuppasatthā, ime dhammā samattā samādinnā hitāya sukhāya saṁvattantī’ti, atha tumhe, bhaddiya, upasampajja vihareyyāthāti.
root_text.an4.193:9.2: alobho purisassa ajjhattaṁ uppajjamāno uppajjati hitāya vā ahitāya vā”ti?
root_text.an4.193:9.4: “Aluddho panāyaṁ, bhaddiya, purisapuggalo lobhena anabhibhūto apariyādinnacitto neva pāṇaṁ hanati, na adinnaṁ ādiyati, na paradāraṁ gacchati, na musā bhaṇati, parampi tathattāya na samādapeti yaṁsa hoti dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāyā”ti.
root_text.an4.193:10.1: “Taṁ kiṁ maññatha, bhaddiya, adoso purisassa …pe… amoho purisassa …pe… asārambho purisassa ajjhattaṁ uppajjamāno uppajjati hitāya vā ahitāya vā”ti?
root_text.an4.193:10.3: “Asāraddho panāyaṁ, bhaddiya, purisapuggalo sārambhena anabhibhūto apariyādinnacitto neva pāṇaṁ hanati, na adinnaṁ ādiyati, na paradāraṁ gacchati, na musā bhaṇati, parampi tathattāya na samādapeti yaṁsa hoti dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāyā”ti.
root_text.an4.193:11.1: “Taṁ kiṁ maññatha, bhaddiya, ime dhammā kusalā vā akusalā vā”ti?
root_text.an4.193:11.7: “Samattā samādinnā hitāya sukhāya saṁvattanti no vā?
root_text.an4.193:12.1: “Iti kho, bhaddiya, yaṁ taṁ te avocumhā—
root_text.an4.193:12.2: etha tumhe, bhaddiya, mā anussavena, mā paramparāya, mā itikirāya, mā piṭakasampadānena, mā takkahetu, mā nayahetu, mā ākāraparivitakkena, mā diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā, mā bhabbarūpatāya, mā ‘samaṇo no garū’ti.
root_text.an4.193:12.4: ‘ime dhammā kusalā, ime dhammā anavajjā, ime dhammā viññuppasatthā, ime dhammā samattā samādinnā hitāya sukhāya saṁvattantīti, atha tumhe, bhaddiya, upasampajja vihareyyāthā’ti,
root_text.an4.193:12.5: iti yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ idametaṁ paṭicca vuttaṁ.
root_text.an4.193:13.1: Ye kho te, bhaddiya, loke santo sappurisā te sāvakaṁ evaṁ samādapenti:
root_text.an4.193:13.2: ‘ehi tvaṁ, ambho purisa, lobhaṁ vineyya viharāhi.
root_text.an4.193:13.3: Lobhaṁ vineyya viharanto na lobhajaṁ kammaṁ karissasi kāyena vācāya manasā.
root_text.an4.193:13.5: Dosaṁ vineyya viharanto na dosajaṁ kammaṁ karissasi kāyena vācāya manasā.
root_text.an4.193:13.7: Mohaṁ vineyya viharanto na mohajaṁ kammaṁ karissasi kāyena vācāya manasā.
root_text.an4.193:13.9: Sārambhaṁ vineyya viharanto na sārambhajaṁ kammaṁ karissasi kāyena vācāya manasā’”ti.
root_text.an4.193:14.1: Evaṁ vutte, bhaddiyo licchavi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.193:14.2: “abhikkantaṁ, bhante …pe… upāsakaṁ maṁ, bhante, bhagavā dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.
root_text.an4.193:15.5: “Evaṁvādiṁ kho maṁ, bhaddiya, evamakkhāyiṁ eke samaṇabrāhmaṇā asatā tucchā musā abhūtena abbhācikkhanti:
root_text.an4.193:15.6: ‘māyāvī samaṇo gotamo āvaṭṭaniṁ māyaṁ jānāti yāya aññatitthiyānaṁ sāvake āvaṭṭetī’”ti.
root_text.an4.193:15.9: Piyā me, bhante, ñātisālohitā imāya āvaṭṭaniyā āvaṭṭeyyuṁ, piyānampi me assa ñātisālohitānaṁ dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāya.
root_text.an4.193:15.10: Sabbe cepi, bhante, khattiyā imāya āvaṭṭaniyā āvaṭṭeyyuṁ, sabbesampissa khattiyānaṁ dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāya.
root_text.an4.193:15.11: Sabbe cepi, bhante, brāhmaṇā … vessā … suddā imāya āvaṭṭaniyā āvaṭṭeyyuṁ, sabbesampissa suddānaṁ dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāyā”ti.
root_text.an4.193:16.2: Sabbe cepi, bhaddiya, khattiyā imāya āvaṭṭaniyā āvaṭṭeyyuṁ akusaladhammappahānāya kusaladhammūpasampadāya, sabbesampissa khattiyānaṁ dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāya.
root_text.an4.193:16.3: Sabbe cepi, bhaddiya, brāhmaṇā … vessā … suddā āvaṭṭeyyuṁ akusaladhammappahānāya kusaladhammūpasampadāya, sabbesampissa suddānaṁ dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāya.
root_text.an4.193:16.4: Sadevako cepi, bhaddiya, loko samārako sabrahmako sassamaṇabrāhmaṇī pajā sadevamanussā imāya āvaṭṭaniyā āvaṭṭeyyuṁ akusaladhammappahānāya kusaladhammūpasampadāya, sadevakassapissa lokassa samārakassa sabrahmakassa sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāya.
root_text.an4.193:16.5: Ime cepi, bhaddiya, mahāsālā imāya āvaṭṭaniyā āvaṭṭeyyuṁ akusaladhammappahānāya kusaladhammūpasampadāya, imesampissa mahāsālānaṁ dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāya (…).

translation_text.an4.193:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.an4.193:1.2: Then Bhaddiya the Licchavi went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an4.193:2.2: ‘The ascetic Gotama is deceitful. He knows a conversion trick, and uses it to convert the disciples of those of other religions.’
translation_text.an4.193:2.4: I trust that those who say this repeat what the Buddha has said, and do not misrepresent him with an untruth? Is their explanation in line with the teaching? Are there any legitimate grounds for rebuttal and criticism?”
translation_text.an4.193:3.1: “Please, Bhaddiya, don’t go by oral transmission, don’t go by lineage, don’t go by testament, don’t go by canonical authority, don’t rely on logic, don’t rely on inference, don’t go by reasoned train of thought, don’t go by the acceptance of a view after deliberation, don’t go by the appearance of competence, and don’t think ‘The ascetic is our respected teacher.’
translation_text.an4.193:3.2: But when you know for yourselves:
translation_text.an4.193:3.3: ‘These things are unskillful, blameworthy, criticized by sensible people, and when you undertake them, they lead to harm and suffering’, then you should give them up.
translation_text.an4.193:4.2: Does greed come up in a person for their welfare or harm?”
translation_text.an4.193:4.4: “A greedy individual—overcome by greed—kills living creatures, steals, commits adultery, lies, and encourages others to do the same. Is that for their lasting harm and suffering?”
translation_text.an4.193:5.2: Does hate … or delusion … or aggression come up in a person for their welfare or harm?”
translation_text.an4.193:5.4: “An aggressive individual kills living creatures, steals, commits adultery, lies, and encourages others to do the same. Is that for their lasting harm and suffering?”
translation_text.an4.193:6.1: “What do you think, Bhaddiya, are these things skillful or unskillful?”
translation_text.an4.193:6.7: “When you undertake them, do they lead to harm and suffering, or not?
translation_text.an4.193:6.8: Or how do you see this?”
translation_text.an4.193:6.9: “When you undertake them, they lead to harm and suffering.
translation_text.an4.193:7.2: ‘Please, Bhaddiya, don’t go by oral transmission, don’t go by lineage, don’t go by testament, don’t go by canonical authority, don’t rely on logic, don’t rely on inference, don’t go by reasoned train of thought, don’t go by the acceptance of a view after deliberation, don’t go by the appearance of competence, and don’t think “The ascetic is our respected teacher.”
translation_text.an4.193:7.3: But when you know for yourselves:
translation_text.an4.193:7.4: “These things are unskillful, blameworthy, criticized by sensible people, and when you undertake them, they lead to harm and suffering”, then you should give them up.’
translation_text.an4.193:7.5: That’s what I said, and this is why I said it.
translation_text.an4.193:8.1: Please, Bhaddiya, don’t rely on oral transmission …
translation_text.an4.193:8.2: But when you know for yourselves:
translation_text.an4.193:8.3: ‘These things are skillful, blameless, praised by sensible people, and when you undertake them, they lead to welfare and happiness’, then you should acquire them and keep them.
translation_text.an4.193:9.2: Does contentment … love … understanding … benevolence come up in a person for their welfare or harm?”
translation_text.an4.193:10.3: “An individual who is benevolent—not overcome by aggression—doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit adultery, lie, or encourage others to do the same. Is that for their lasting welfare and happiness?”
translation_text.an4.193:11.1: “What do you think, Bhaddiya, are these things skillful or unskillful?”
translation_text.an4.193:11.7: “When you undertake them, do they lead to welfare and happiness, or not?
translation_text.an4.193:11.8: Or how do you see this?”
translation_text.an4.193:11.9: “When you undertake them, they lead to welfare and happiness.
translation_text.an4.193:12.2: ‘Please, Bhaddiya, don’t go by oral transmission, don’t go by lineage, don’t go by testament, don’t go by canonical authority, don’t rely on logic, don’t rely on inference, don’t go by reasoned train of thought, don’t go by the acceptance of a view after deliberation, don’t go by the appearance of competence, and don’t think “The ascetic is our respected teacher.”
translation_text.an4.193:12.3: But when you know for yourselves:
translation_text.an4.193:12.4: “These things are skillful, blameless, praised by sensible people, and when you undertake them, they lead to welfare and happiness”, then you should acquire them and keep them.’
translation_text.an4.193:12.5: That’s what I said, and this is why I said it.
translation_text.an4.193:13.1: The good and true persons in the world encourage their disciples:
translation_text.an4.193:13.2: ‘Please, my friend, live rid of greed.
translation_text.an4.193:13.3: Then you won’t act out of greed by way of body, speech, or mind.
translation_text.an4.193:13.4: Live rid of hate … delusion … aggression.
translation_text.an4.193:13.5: Then you won’t act out of hate … delusion … aggression by way of body, speech, or mind.”
translation_text.an4.193:14.1: When he said this, Bhaddiya the Licchavi said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an4.193:14.2: “Excellent, sir! … From this day forth, may the Buddha remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
translation_text.an4.193:15.1: “Well, Bhaddiya, did I say to you:
translation_text.an4.193:15.3: and I will be your teacher’?”
translation_text.an4.193:15.5: “Though I state and assert this, certain ascetics and brahmins misrepresent me with the incorrect, hollow, false, untruthful claim:
translation_text.an4.193:15.6: ‘The ascetic Gotama is deceitful. He knows a conversion trick, and uses it to convert the disciples of those of other religions.’”
translation_text.an4.193:15.9: If my loved ones—relatives and kin—were to be converted by this, it would be for their lasting welfare and happiness.
translation_text.an4.193:15.10: If all the aristocrats, brahmins, peasants, and menials were to be converted by this, it would be for their lasting welfare and happiness.”
translation_text.an4.193:16.1: “That’s so true, Bhaddiya! That’s so true, Bhaddiya!
translation_text.an4.193:16.2: If all the aristocrats, brahmins, peasants, and menials were to be converted by this, it would be for their lasting welfare and happiness.
translation_text.an4.193:16.4: If the whole world—with its gods, Māras, and divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, gods and humans—were to be converted by this, for giving up unskillful qualities and embracing skillful qualities, it would be for their lasting welfare and happiness.
translation_text.an4.193:16.5: If these great sal trees were to be converted by this, for giving up unskillful qualities and embracing skillful qualities, it would be for their lasting welfare and happiness—if they were sentient.
translation_text.an4.193:16.6: How much more then a human being!”

variant_text.an4.193:2.2: āvaṭṭaniṁ māyaṁ → āvaṭṭanīmāyaṁ (bj); āvaṭṭanimāyaṁ (sya-all, km, mr)
variant_text.an4.193:13.2: lobhaṁ vineyya → vineyya vineyya (bj, sya-all, km)
variant_text.an4.193:13.3: Lobhaṁ vineyya → vineyya vineyya (bj, sya-all, km)
